title: T-110 — PostHog Feature Flags Proxy (SET-072 §10.3)
prompt:
# T-110 — PostHog Feature Flags Proxy (SET-072 §10.3)

PRD: 02-SETTINGS-PRD.md §10.3 (Module Toggles + Feature Flags UI surfaces)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement the read-through proxy endpoint that fetches PostHog feature flags scoped to the caller's org and returns a normalized payload for the SET-072 client. Built as a Next.js Route Handler (App Router) that calls PostHog's decide API server-side using a server-only API key, never exposing it to the browser. Cached per-org for 60s.

## Implementation contract
1. Route handler at apps/web/app/api/posthog/flags/route.ts (GET) — uses POSTHOG_PROJECT_API_KEY from process.env (server-only).
2. Resolve caller org_id via withOrgContext (T-015); reject if not authenticated.
3. Call PostHog decide API (`POST {POSTHOG_HOST}/decide?v=3` with `{api_key, distinct_id, groups: { organization: org_id }}`).
4. Cache normalized response (Map<org_id, {flags, fetched_at}>) for 60 seconds via in-memory LRU; serve cached on hit.
5. Return JSON `{ flags: Record<string, boolean | string>, fetched_at: ISO8601 }`. Errors return 502 with `{ error_code: 'POSTHOG_UPSTREAM_ERROR' }` and log via tenant audit.

## Files
- apps/web/app/api/posthog/flags/route.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/api/posthog/flags/route.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` array.

## Test strategy
1. RED: vitest mocks `fetch` to PostHog decide endpoint; asserts org_id forwarded as `groups.organization`, response normalized, cache hit on second call within 60s.
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/api/posthog/flags/route.test.ts`
2. RED must fail.
3. GREEN: implement route + LRU cache + error mapping.
4. Re-run RED + `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not expose POSTHOG_PROJECT_API_KEY to client bundles; reference only via process.env in server file.
- Do not bypass withOrgContext (RLS / multi-tenant isolation).
- Do not mirror PostHog flags into feature_flags_core table (per T-013 risk_red_line).

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router (Route Handler) + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + Server Actions. PRD: 02-SETTINGS-PRD.md v3.5.

pipeline_inputs.description: Read-through Next.js Route Handler proxying PostHog decide API per-org with 60s LRU cache and audit on upstream error.
pipeline_inputs.details: PRD §10.3 SET-072 `Read-through view PostHog flags per org`. The proxy is the prerequisite backend for the future SET-072 view component (UI deferred). API key is server-only — never imported in `'use client'` modules. Cache is per-process LRU; 60s TTL acceptable for admin viewer use case. Upstream errors logged via tenant audit so SRE can alert.
out_of_scope: SET-072 client component (separate T3 task, deferred)
Write/override flag values via UI (PostHog UI is source of truth)
acceptance_criteria: Given the caller is authenticated with org_id='org_42', when GET /api/posthog/flags is called, then the upstream POST to PostHog decide includes `groups.organization='org_42'` and the response is returned as `{ flags, fetched_at }`.
Given a successful response was cached at t0, when GET /api/posthog/flags is called again at t0+30s, then no second upstream fetch is issued and the cached payload is returned.
Given PostHog returns 5xx or times out, when the handler runs, then it returns 502 with `{ error_code: 'POSTHOG_UPSTREAM_ERROR' }` and emits an audit_log entry.
Given the caller is unauthenticated, when GET /api/posthog/flags is called, then 401 is returned and no upstream fetch is issued.
test_strategy: RED: vitest mocks global fetch; case 1 happy path asserts decide payload + groups.organization; case 2 cache hit asserts only one fetch in 60s window; case 3 5xx returns 502 + audit; case 4 unauth returns 401.
GREEN: implement route + LRU + audit emit.
risk_red_lines: Do not expose POSTHOG_PROJECT_API_KEY to client bundles; reference only via process.env in server file.
Do not bypass withOrgContext (RLS / multi-tenant isolation).
Do not mirror PostHog flags into feature_flags_core (per T-013 risk_red_line).
